steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank, “has been displayed at the Smithsonian American Art Museum” is an independent clause. After the blank, “which features a large collection of contemporary art” is a relative clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for relative clauses
steps.content: A comma precedes a non-restrictive relative clause that provides additional information. Choice D correctly places a comma before “which features a large collection of contemporary art.” The other choices fail to follow this rule: choice A uses “and which” (non-standard coordination), choice B uses a semicolon before a relative clause, and choice C starts a sentence with a relative pronoun. That matches choice D — D is correct.
